title: Mind Reader at the White House? Oz Pearlman to Perform at Big Dinner
summary:
What if someone could read your mind—and then tell everyone in the room what you were thinking? Creepy? Cool? Both?

Well, that's exactly what's happening at the White House Correspondents' Dinner this Saturday! Instead of a comedian telling jokes and roasting (making fun of) politicians, a "mentalist" named Oz Pearlman will be the star. A mentalist is a kind of magician who seems to read people's minds. Pearlman is famous for guessing people's PIN numbers, secrets, and even what they're thinking—and he's gone viral on social media for it.

This dinner is a big deal. It's been around for over 100 years, and usually a comedian makes jokes about the president and politicians. But this year, they wanted something different. Pearlman says his job is to "bring us together" and make people laugh—even if they disagree on everything else.

And here's the really cool part: President Trump will be there! Pearlman hints that Trump won't just be watching—he'll be part of the act. "Reading Donald Trump's mind is arguably the most impressive thing you could ever do," Pearlman says. He's been planning this moment for 10 years.

Can he really do it? How do mentalists "read minds" anyway? They use research, suggestion, and showmanship (a fancy word for putting on a good show). They don't actually read your thoughts—but it sure looks like they do!

Will Pearlman wow the crowd? We'll find out Saturday night. One thing's for sure: everyone will be asking, "How did he do that?"
why_it_matters: This story shows how entertainment can bring people with different opinions together to laugh.
